steps.title: Keep the jugglers plural
steps.content: The sentence refers to "two jugglers" and says the laws of physics are identical for both of them. The blank therefore needs the plural noun "jugglers," not the singular possessive "juggler's" or plural possessive "jugglers'."

steps.title: Use the possessive pronoun
steps.content: Choice A uses possessive "jugglers'" and the contraction "they're." Choice B uses "there," and choice C uses singular possessive "juggler's." The sentence needs plural "jugglers" plus possessive "their" before "motion." The answer is D.
